job_snippets: Design and implement scalable cloud infrastructure on AWS, Azure, or GCP using IaC tools like Terraform or CloudFormation.
Manage Kubernetes clusters and containerized workloads with Docker; ensure high availability and fault tolerance.
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or GitLab CI to automate deployments.
Monitor cloud resources using Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, or CloudWatch; configure alerts and dashboards.
Implement security best practices: IAM roles, VPC networking, encryption at rest and in transit.
Optimize cloud costs through right-sizing, reserved instances, and resource tagging strategies.
Configure auto-scaling groups, load balancers, and CDN distributions for global workloads.
Write Infrastructure as Code using Terraform or Pulumi; manage state files and module reuse.
Administer Linux servers: user management, firewall rules, cron jobs, systemd services.
Design multi-region disaster recovery architectures with RTO/RPO targets.
Implement logging pipelines using ELK stack or cloud-native log aggregation services.
Collaborate with developers to containerize applications and define Helm charts for Kubernetes.
Perform security audits, manage secrets with HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager.
Experience with serverless architectures: AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, Google Cloud Functions.
Proficiency in scripting languages (Python, Bash, Go) for automation and tooling.
Manage DNS, SSL certificates, and networking with Route 53, Cloudflare, or Azure DNS.
Configure service meshes (Istio, Linkerd) for microservices observability and traffic management.
Participate in on-call rotations; conduct post-mortems and reliability improvements.
Work with databases in cloud: RDS, Aurora, Cosmos DB, Firestore, Redis, DynamoDB.
Strong understanding of networking: TCP/IP, HTTP/HTTPS, BGP, VPN, Direct Connect.
